keywords.label: 삼성전자 2Q 실적
keywords.desc: 오늘 장중 잠정실적 발표 — 영업이익 컨센서스 84~85조원, 최대 90.2조원 전망. 코스피 8,000선 사수 분수령

keywords.label: 다우 53,000 돌파
keywords.desc: 사상 첫 53,000선 돌파. AI 우려 진정 속 기술주 랠리 재개. 테슬라 +6.7%, 메타 +3.0%

keywords.label: MS 4,800명 감원
keywords.desc: 전체 인력 2.1%·X박스 20% 감축. AI 전환 가속화 신호. 빅테크 AI 투자 $7,000억↑

keywords.label: SK하이닉스 ADR
keywords.desc: 7월 10일 나스닥 정식 상장 예정. 43조원 규모. 국내 반도체 업종 재평가 기대

keywords.label: FOMC 의사록
keywords.desc: 8일(내일) 6월 FOMC 의사록 공개. 워시 신임 의장 첫 의사록. 금리 경로 단서 주목

domestic.comment: 전주 극심한 변동성(7/2 -7.89% → 7/3 +5.76%) 이후 안정화 국면. 반도체 대형주 반등 시도했으나 외국인·기관 동반 매도로 소폭 하락. 코스닥 2%대 하락.

morning.topNews: 삼성전자 2분기 잠정실적 발표 D-DAY — 영업이익 컨센서스 84~85조원(+1,700%↑), DS 특별성과급 10~18조원, 충당 전 DS 영업익 109.5조원 추정. DRAM ASP +45%, NAND +65% QoQ.
otherNews: MS 대규모 감원 (4,800명 / X박스 20% 감축) — AI 전환 가속화 신호
미·이란 종전 협상 진전 — 트럼프 '합의 안 하면 폭격' 경고 속 협상

mailCheck.content: Vercel 새 로그인 감지 알림 — 보안 확인 필요
Harvard Online AI Leadership Series 신규 강좌 개설
삼성증권 유통업종 리포트 — 부의 효과와 인바운드 수요 증가
ZDNet Korea 반도체 추가 세수로 미래대응기금 조성
Anthropic Claude 보안 로그인 링크 2건 (본인 확인됨)

morning.insight: 삼성전자 실적의 날. 판을 읽는 것이 중요하다. 컨센서스 84~85조, KB증권 90.2조. 충당금 10~18조를 떼고도 이 정도. 이미 기대가 많이 반영된 상태에서 '재료 소멸'로 작용할지 '어닝 서프라이즈 모멘텀'으로 이어질지는 오늘 장을 지켜봐야. 해외는 다우 사상 첫 53,000선 돌파, AI 우려 진정. 체크 포인트: 삼성전자 실적, 외국인 수급 전환, 8일 FOMC 의사록, SK하이닉스 ADR 상장(7/10).

articles.headline: 트럼프 "지금이 美황금기 시작"…트럼프 어카운트 출범
articles.eng: Trump Declares 'Golden Age of America' Begins with Trump Accounts Launch
articles.body: 트럼프 대통령이 7월 4일 독립기념일 연설에서 트럼프 어카운트 출범을 선언하며 '미국 황금기의 시작'이라고 강조. 모든 미국 아동에게 $1,000 종잣돈을 제공하는 세제혜택 계좌로, 출범 첫 주에만 8억 달러 규모의 신규 자금이 주식시장에 유입될 전망.

articles.headline: AI·OTT 월정액 달러유출 '눈덩이'…연간 50억 달러 육박
articles.eng: AI and OTT Subscription Dollar Outflows Surge Toward $5B Annually
articles.body: 국내에서 AI 서비스 및 OTT 플랫폼 구독료로 빠져나가는 달러가 연간 50억 달러에 육박. OpenAI, Microsoft, Netflix 등 글로벌 빅테크로의 디지털 서비스 무역적자가 사상 최대치를 기록 중.

articles.headline: "변동성 통제불가 수준"…괴물된 삼닉, 코스피 8,000 사수
articles.eng: Volatility Reaches Uncontrollable Levels as Samsung-SK Hynix Defend KOSPI 8,000
articles.body: 전주 코스피가 하루 7.89% 폭락 후 다음날 5.76% 반등하는 등 극심한 변동성 장세. 개인투자자 2.6조 순매수로 8,000선 방어. 오늘 삼성전자 2Q 잠정실적 발표가 최대 변수.

articles.headline: [단독] "10㎡ 사무실이 1주택?"…토허제가 만든 황당 다주택자
articles.eng: Exclusive: 10m² Office Counts as One Home Under Land Transaction Permit System
articles.body: 토지거래허가제로 인해 소형 사무실도 주택으로 간주되어 예상치 못한 다주택자가 양산되는 문제 발생. 업계에서는 제도 개선을 촉구.

articles.headline: 20년 넘은 맛집마저 무너졌다…장기 불황에 자영업자 줄폐업
articles.eng: Even 20-Year-Old Restaurants Collapse as Long Recession Drives Mass Closures
articles.body: 지난해 폐업 신고한 개인·법인 사업자 수가 사상 처음 100만명을 돌파. 20년 이상 영업한 노포 맛집들도 장기 불황을 견디지 못하고 문을 닫는 사례 증가.

articles.headline: "기업 무너지면 은행 독박"…환헤지 파생계약 폭증 금융권 비상
articles.eng: Banks Face Risk as Currency Hedge Derivatives Surge Amid Corporate Distress
articles.body: 환율 변동성이 커지면서 기업들의 환헤지 파생계약이 폭증. 금융권에서는 기업이 무너질 경우 은행이 대규모 손실을 떠안을 수 있다는 우려가 커지고 있음.

articles.headline: 좋은 일 하나 없다…내우외환 시달리는 한국 기업들, 실적 쇼크 시작
articles.eng: Korean Companies Hit by Triple Whammy as Earnings Shock Begins
articles.body: LG전자 2Q 영업이익 46.6% 급감. 미국 관세, 수요 부진, 물류비 증가 3중고. 삼성전자·현대차·기아도 2Q 실적 악화 예상. 하반기 관세 영향 본격화 경고.

articles.headline: 반도체 '날개'에도 고용 부진…고용탄성치 8년 만에 최저
articles.eng: Employment Elasticity Hits 8-Year Low Despite Semiconductor Boom
articles.body: 반도체 초호황으로 경제성장률 2.5% 전망되나 고용탄성치는 0.24로 2018년 이후 최저. AI 확산과 자본집약적 반도체 산업 특성상 고용 창출력 약화. 청년층 타격 가장 커.

articles.headline: "주식으로 번 돈 부동산으로"…5대 은행 집단대출 올해 15조↑
articles.eng: Stock Market Gains Flow Into Real Estate as Group Loans Surge 15 Trillion Won
articles.body: 주식시장 활황으로 얻은 수익이 부동산 시장으로 유입되며 5대 은행 집단대출이 올해 들어 15조원 이상 증가. '동탄 막히자 평택으로' 풍선효과도 나타나는 중.

articles.headline: "트럼프 만세" "2조 벌 듯"…전쟁 틈타 14조 유가담합 '폭리'
articles.eng: Refiners Indicted for 14 Trillion Won Price-Fixing During Iran War, Mocked 'Trump Hooray'
articles.body: 검찰, 미·이란 전쟁 직후 유가 담합으로 14조원 폭리 취한 HD현대오일뱅크 등 정유사 기소. 내부 단톡방에서 '트럼프 만세', '올해 2조 벌 듯' 등의 대화 정황 포착. 증거인멸 정황도.

articles.headline: 외환 24시간 거래 첫날…환율 1,312원, 야간 변동성 제한적
articles.eng: 24-Hour FX Trading Debuts With Limited Overnight Volatility at 1,312 Won
articles.body: 외환시장 24시간 거래 첫날 원/달러 환율 1,312원으로 마감. 야간 거래량은 예상보다 적었고 변동성도 제한적. 시장 참가자들은 점진적 적응 예상.

articles.headline: 국민연금 '삼전닉스 매도 폭탄' 막는다…與 리밸런싱 제한 추진
articles.eng: Ruling Party Moves to Block National Pension's Samsung-SK Hynix Sell-Off
articles.body: 국민연금이 삼성전자·SK하이닉스 보유 비중이 상한선을 초과하자 여당이 리밸런싱(매도) 제한을 추진. 국민연금 4개월간 340조원 폭증하며 운용 규모 1,470조원 돌파.

articles.headline: 이재명 대통령 "역사적 과제에 협조 못해도 방해는 말아야"…야당 메가프로젝트 비판에 일침
articles.eng: President Lee Says Opposition Should Not Obstruct Historic Mega Projects
articles.body: 이재명 대통령이 3대 메가프로젝트 관련 야당 비판에 '왜 우리는 빠졌냐고 항의하더니 같은 입으로 사기라고 한다'며 강하게 반박. '최대한 협조는 못하더라도 방해는 안 했으면 좋겠다'고 말해.

articles.headline: 트럼프, 나토 정상회의 참석…유럽 동맹국에 '충성심' 요구
articles.eng: Trump Demands 'Loyalty' From European Allies at NATO Summit in Turkey
articles.body: 트럼프 대통령이 7일 터키에서 열리는 나토 정상회의에 참석. 미·이란 전쟁에서 지원하지 않은 유럽 동맹국에 방위비 증액과 충성심을 요구할 전망.

articles.headline: 7월 임시국회 '반쪽' 개회…與 상임위 가동·국힘 보이콧
articles.eng: July Extra Session Opens as Half-Parliament With Ruling Party Driving, Opposition Boycotting
articles.body: 민주당 소집 요구로 7월 임시국회 개회했으나 국민의힘 불참 속 '반쪽 국회'. 민주당은 메가특구 특별법 등 민생경제 법안 처리 시급 강조. 국민의힘은 법사위원장직 양보가 전제돼야 한다는 강경론.

articles.headline: 투표용지 부족 사태…선관위 "재발 방지 대책 마련"
articles.eng: Ballot Paper Shortage Scandal — Election Commission Promises Preventive Measures
articles.body: 최근 선거에서 발생한 투표용지 부족 사태에 대해 중앙선거관리위원회가 재발 방지 대책을 마련 중. 야당은 선관위 특검법을 요구하며 공세 강화.

articles.headline: 홈플러스 파산 위기…카드사 대금보류 통보에 유동성 경색
articles.eng: Homeplus Faces Bankruptcy Crisis as Credit Card Companies Halt Payments
articles.body: 벼랑 끝에 몰린 홈플러스에 카드사들이 대금 보류를 통보. 유동성 경색이 심화되며 파산 위기 고조. 대형마트 업계 전반의 구조조정 가능성에 시장 긴장.

articles.headline: 김민석, '당정일치'로 정청래 직격…민주당 당권 레이스 점화
articles.eng: Kim Min-seok Targets Chung Cheong-rae Over Party-Government Unity in Leadership Race
articles.body: 김민석 전 국무총리가 8·17 전당대회 출마 선언. '완벽한 당정일치와 민생·실용·통합 노선' 강조하며 정청래 전 대표의 '자기정치' 비판. 친청계 이성윤 의원 즉각 반발. 송영길 의원 8일 출마선언 예정.

articles.headline: 이재명·한성숙 첫 주례회동…"AI 대전환·규제합리화 전권 갖고 추진"
articles.eng: President Lee and PM Han Hold First Weekly Meeting, Push AI Transformation
articles.body: 이재명 대통령과 한성숙 국무총리가 첫 주례보고회동. 'AI 대전환'과 '규제합리화'를 총리 4대 역점 아젠다로 설정. 이 대통령은 기술로 국민 생명을 지킬 획기적 방안 마련 지시.

articles.headline: 장동혁 "입틀막법은 이재명 독재 완성"…국힘 보이콧 의지 재확인
articles.eng: Opposition Leader Calls Info-Communications Law 'Completion of Lee Jae-myung Dictatorship'
articles.body: 장동혁 국민의힘 대표가 개정 정보통신망법을 '입틀막법'으로 규정. "반중 언론 문 닫고 감옥 보낸 홍콩의 모습이 떠오른다"며 "그 끝은 이재명 독재의 완성"이라고 강도 높게 비판.

articles.headline: '5·18 성역' 발언 이병태 부위원장, 靑 권고에 결국 사퇴
articles.eng: Lee Byung-tae Resigns Over 'May 18 Sanctuary' Remarks After Presidential Office Recommendation
articles.body: 5·18 민주화운동을 '성역'으로 표현해 논란을 일으킨 이병태 부위원장이 청와대의 사퇴 권고를 받아들여 결국 물러나. 이 전 부위원장은 "성역 강요하는 사회는 안 된다"는 입장을 고수했으나 결국 사퇴.

articles.headline: 나토 벽 높았다…캐나다, 60조 잠수함 사업자에 獨 TKMS 선정
articles.eng: NATO Wall Too High — Canada Picks Germany's TKMS for $40B Submarine Project
articles.body: 캐나다가 60조원 규모의 차세대 잠수함 사업자로 독일 TKMS를 선정. 한국 한화오션과 HD현대중공업이 수주 경쟁에 나섰으나 나토 회원국 간 방산 협력 벽을 넘지 못해 고배.

articles.headline: 정몽규 체제 13년만에 끝…K-축구혁신위 박지성 "회장 선거제도 개선"
articles.eng: Chung Mong-gyu's 13-Year Reign Ends — Park Ji-sung Leads K-Football Reform Committee
articles.body: 정몽규 대한축구협회장이 13년 만에 물러나고 K-축구혁신위원회가 공식 출범. 박지성 위원장이 회장 선거제도 개선을 최우선 과제로 제시하며 축구계 쇄신 예고.

articles.headline: 광주 군공항 부지에 삼성-SK 반도체 '메가 팹' 짓는다
articles.eng: Samsung-SK Semiconductor Mega Fab to Be Built on Gwangju Military Airport Site
articles.body: 호남권 반도체 메가 클러스터의 핵심인 메가 팹이 광주 군공항 이전 부지에 들어선다. 이재명 대통령의 3대 메가프로젝트 일환. 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스가 공동 투자.

articles.headline: 월드컵에 뜬 현대차 아틀라스…심판에 공 전달 쇼
articles.eng: Hyundai Atlas Appears at World Cup With Ball Delivery Show to Referee
articles.body: 2026 북중미 월드컵에서 현대차의 자율주행 로봇 '아틀라스'가 개막식에서 심판에게 공을 전달하는 퍼포먼스로 세계적 주목. 현대차의 로보틱스 기술력 과시.

articles.headline: 오늘부터 허위·조작정보 고의 유통 땐 최대 5배 손해배상
articles.eng: Up to 5x Damages for Intentional Spread of False Information Starting Today
articles.body: 개정 정보통신망법 시행으로 허위·조작정보를 고의로 유통할 경우 최대 5배 징벌적 손해배상. 야당은 '입틀막법'으로 비판하며 위헌 소송 예고.

articles.headline: 중동전쟁 틈타 기름값 40% 올린 정유사…"트럼프 만세" 조롱까지
articles.eng: Refiners Raised Oil Prices 40% During War, Mocked 'Trump Hooray' in Chat
articles.body: 검찰 수사 결과 미·이란 전쟁 직후 정유4사가 담합해 기름값을 최대 40% 인상. HD현대오일뱅크와 SK에너지가 가격 정보 교환. 내부 단톡방에서 '트럼프 만세' '2조 벌 듯' 등 발언.

articles.headline: 3군 사관학교 통합 계획, 100분 전 돌연 취소한 국방부
articles.eng: Defense Ministry Abruptly Cancels Tri-Service Academy Integration Plan 100 Minutes Before Announcement
articles.body: 육·해·공군 사관학교 통합 계획 발표 100분 전 국방부가 돌연 취소. 군 내부 반발과 정치적 부담이 원인으로 지목. 국방부는 '추가 검토 필요' 입장.

articles.headline: 이재명 대통령 "3대 메가 시간낭비 말아야…환경평가 기존결과 원용"
articles.eng: President Lee Orders Speed on Mega Projects, Use Existing Environmental Assessments
articles.body: 이 대통령이 메가프로젝트 민관합동 점검회의에서 '오직 속도전'을 강조하며 환경평가 등 행정절차에 기존 결과를 원용하라고 지시. "불법 아니면 모두 다"라며 전폭적 지원 약속.

articles.headline: 이재명 지지율 7주 만에 소폭 반등 47%…'3대 메가'로 반등
articles.eng: President Lee's Approval Rebounds to 47% After 7 Weeks on Mega Project Push
articles.body: 리얼미터 조사 결과 이재명 대통령 지지율이 7주 만에 소폭 반등한 47%. 민주당 지지율 3주 연속 상승, 국민의힘 3주 연속 하락. 3대 메가프로젝트 추진이 반등 요인으로 분석.

articles.headline: 7월 임시국회 개막…與 "메가 특별법부터" vs 野 "입틀막법 위헌"
articles.eng: July Extra Session Opens With Ruling Party Pushing Mega Bills, Opposition Crying Unconstitutional
articles.body: 민주당은 메가특구 특별법 등 민생경제 법안의 조속한 처리를 강조하며 TF를 특별위원회로 격상. 국민의힘은 개정 정보통신망법을 '입틀막법'으로 규정하고 강하게 반발.

articles.headline: 한국, 림팩 훈련서 아시아 최초 연합해군구성군사령관 임무
articles.eng: South Korea First Asian Nation to Lead Combined Naval Force at RIMPAC 2026
articles.body: 한국 해군이 2026년 림팩(RIMPAC) 훈련에서 아시아 국가 최초로 연합해군구성군사령관(CFMCC) 임무를 맡아. 한국 해군의 위상과 작전 능력을 국제적으로 인정받은 성과.

articles.headline: "'괴물'을 증명하다"…삼성전자 오늘 2Q 잠정실적 발표
articles.eng: Proving the Monster — Samsung Electronics Q2 Preliminary Earnings Today
articles.body: 삼성전자가 오늘 2분기 잠정실적 발표. 영업이익 컨센서스 85조원, KB증권 90.2조원 전망. DS부문 충당금 10~18조 반영 전 실적은 100조원 돌파 예상. 코스피 8,000선 사수 분수령.

articles.headline: 삼성전자 상반기 성과급, 반도체 최대 100%…스마트폰·가전은 0%
articles.eng: Samsung H1 Bonus: Semiconductor Up to 100%, Smartphone and Home Appliance 0%
articles.body: 삼성전자 상반기 성과급이 DS(반도체) 부문은 기본급의 최대 100%인 반면, MX(스마트폰)와 CE(가전) 부문은 0%로 확정. 반도체 초호황과 스마트폰·가전 부진의 극명한 대비.

articles.headline: 국민연금 4개월간 340조 폭증…1,470조 시대, 반도체 랠리 수혜
articles.eng: National Pension Surges 340 Trillion Won in 4 Months to 1,470 Trillion on Chip Rally
articles.body: 국민연금 운용 규모가 반도체 랠리에 힘입어 4개월 만에 340조원 증가, 1,470조원 돌파. 삼성전자·SK하이닉스 보유 지분 가치 급등이 주요 요인. 포트폴리오 리밸런싱 이슈 부각.

articles.headline: [D-DAY] 삼성전자 2Q 잠정실적 오늘 발표…영업이익 85조원 전망, '괴물 실적' 증명할까
articles.eng: D-DAY: Samsung Q2 Earnings Today — Will It Prove 'Monster Performance' With 85 Trillion Won Profit?
articles.body: 삼성전자 2분기 잠정실적 발표일. 증권사 컨센서스 매출 173조원·영업이익 85조원(+1,700%↑). DS부문 충당 전 영업이익은 109조원 추정. 오늘 실적이 코스피 8,000선 사수 여부 결정.

articles.headline: SK하이닉스 ADR, 7월 10일 나스닥 정식 상장…43조원 규모
articles.eng: SK Hynix ADR to List on Nasdaq July 10 in $28 Billion Offering
articles.body: SK하이닉스가 43조원 규모의 미국 ADR 상장을 7월 10일 추진. 주요 기관투자자로부터 7조원 이상의 사전 수요 확보. 국내 반도체 업종 전반의 재평가 계기로 작용할 전망.

articles.headline: 반도체·車, 차기 주도주…관세 협상 결과에 달렸다
articles.eng: Semiconductors and Autos as Next Market Leaders Hinge on Tariff Negotiations
articles.body: 코스피 3,000선 돌파 이후 숨 고르기 속 증권가 다음 주도주 찾기 분주. 관세 협상 결과에 따라 반도체·자동차 또는 내수·금융 업종이 상승장 이끌 전망. 트럼프 관세 유예 8월 1일 연장.

articles.headline: 외환 24시간 거래 첫날…원화 강세 속 안정적 출발
articles.eng: 24-Hour FX Trading Debuts With Stable Start Amid Won Strength
articles.body: 외환시장 24시간 거래제가 첫날을 맞아 안정적으로 출발. 원/달러 환율 1,312원. 미·이란 종전 협상 진전과 위험선호 심리 회복으로 원화 강세 기조.

articles.headline: 금융지주 '밸류업' 선두…하반기 자사주 1.6조 매입·소각
articles.eng: Financial Holding Companies Lead Value-Up With 1.6 Trillion Won Share Buyback
articles.body: KB·신한·하나·우리 4대 금융지주가 하반기 최소 1조 6,500억원 규모 자사주 매입·소각 예정. KB금융 총주주환원율 54% 전망. CET1 비율 초과분을 주주환원에 적극 활용.

articles.headline: 코스피, 관세 불확실성에도 강보합…내수·금융주 강세
articles.eng: KOSPI Holds Steady Despite Tariff Uncertainty; Domestic and Financial Stocks Strong
articles.body: 코스피가 미국 관세 불확실성 속에서도 내수·금융주 중심으로 강보합. 음식료/담배 +3.05%, 보험 +1.88%. 삼성전자 -1.58%, SK하이닉스 -0.74%로 반도체는 차익실현. 개인·기관 순매수.

articles.headline: 반도체·車 차기 주도주 분석…"관세 피해주 반등 가능성"
articles.eng: Analysts See Rebound Potential in Tariff-Impacted Semiconductor and Auto Stocks
articles.body: 대신증권 "트럼프의 또 한 번 후퇴(TACO) 가능성 높아…실적 대비 낙폭 과대 업종인 반도체와 자동차 주목". DB증권 "대외 불확실성보다 탄탄한 내수에 중점". KB증권 "저PBR 금융주 시대".

articles.headline: 고용 없는 성장 현실화…청년 취업자 5개월 연속 마이너스
articles.eng: Jobless Growth Materializes — Youth Employment Falls for 5 Consecutive Months
articles.body: 15~29세 취업자 수가 올해 1~5월 내내 전년 동월 대비 감소. AI 확산으로 신규 채용 줄고 경력직만 선호. 반도체 호황에도 고용 창출력 약화. 청년 채용 기업 임금 보조 등 대책 시급.

articles.headline: ETF 시장 개인 순매수 '톱10' 싹쓸이…국내 증시 신뢰 회복
articles.eng: Retail Investors Dominate ETF Top 10 Buys as Trust in Domestic Market Recovers
articles.body: 국내 주식형 ETF 상품이 개인투자자 순매수 상위 10개를 휩쓸어. 새 정부 증시 부양책과 주주환원 정책 확대 기대감으로 국내 주가지수 연일 상승세.

articles.headline: 국민연금 '삼전닉스 매도 폭탄' 막는다…與 리밸런싱 제한 추진
articles.eng: Ruling Party Seeks to Block National Pension's Forced Selling of Samsung and SK Hynix
articles.body: 국민연금이 삼성전자·SK하이닉스 보유 비중이 운용 규정 상한을 초과하면서 매도가 불가피한 상황. 여당이 시장 충격을 우려해 리밸런싱 제한을 추진. 국민연금 1,470조원 시대.

articles.headline: [단독] 30대 가계빚, 12년 만에 최대…"주담대·신용대출 동시 증가"
articles.eng: Exclusive: Household Debt for 30-Somethings Hits 12-Year High on Mortgage and Credit Loans
articles.body: 30대 가계부채가 주택담보대출과 신용대출 동시 증가로 12년 만에 최대치. 주식시장 활황으로 형성된 자산 효과가 오히려 레버리지 투자로 이어지는 역설적 현상.

articles.headline: 벼랑 끝 홈플러스, 카드사 '대금보류' 통보…유통업계 지각변동
articles.eng: Homeplus on the Brink as Credit Card Companies Halt Payments — Retail Industry Reshuffle
articles.body: 홈플러스에 카드사들이 대금 보류를 통보하며 유동성 위기 심화. 대형마트 업계 구조조정 가속화 전망. 한때 없어서 못 사는 땅이었던 골프장도 매물로 나와.

articles.headline: Dow Tops 53,000 as Chip Stocks Re-Take the Lead
articles.body: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at a record 53,032.55, up 0.25%, extending a rally that has carried the blue-chip index to a series of fresh highs. Technology shares rebounded from a late-June slump as AI fears eased. The S&P 500 rose 0.72% and Nasdaq climbed 1.12%. Markets are looking forward to Q2 earnings season.

articles.headline: Microsoft to Cut 4,800 Jobs in 'Significant Restructure' of Xbox and Sales
articles.body: Microsoft is cutting about 2.1% of its workforce, or roughly 4,800 jobs, including 1,600 at Xbox immediately with plans to eliminate 20% of gaming staff by fiscal year-end. Amy Coleman, chief people officer, said roles 'are not being replaced by AI' but that AI is 'changing how work gets done.' Four Xbox studios being sold off.

articles.headline: Trump Rings Opening Bell for Trump Accounts From Oval Office
articles.body: President Trump rang the NYSE and NASDAQ opening bells from the Oval Office to mark the launch of Trump Accounts. Every American child receives a $1,000 seed investment. Over 6 million accounts already requested, with 86% from families earning under $200,000. Michael and Susan Dell pledged $6.25 billion.

articles.headline: Trump Heads to NATO Summit in Turkey as Russia Hammers Kyiv
articles.body: President Trump will attend a NATO summit in Turkey this week. Russia launched waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine, killing at least 22 people in Kyiv region. Zelenskyy expected to press allies for more Patriot interceptor missiles at the summit.

articles.headline: Oil Returns to Pre-War Levels as Hormuz Flows Resume After US-Iran Deal
articles.body: Brent crude settled around $72, near pre-Iran war levels, as the Strait of Hormuz reopened following the US-Iran memorandum of understanding. The world absorbed the loss of over a billion barrels of oil supply, but depleted buffer reserves still pose risks of future price spikes.

articles.headline: Fed's Waller Says Forward Guidance 'More Art Than Science'
articles.body: Fed Governor Christopher Waller said forward guidance can strengthen policymaking but has also hindered it, citing the 2021 delay in rate increases. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is forming a communications task force to review practices. FOMC minutes from June meeting to be released Wednesday.

articles.headline: US Economy 'On Wobbly Footing' — Why Wall Street Strategists Are Cautious
articles.body: Despite record highs for S&P 500 and Dow, Wall Street pros signal caution. JPMorgan warns of 'Goldilocks scenario' risks. Comerica's Bill Adams says economy entering H2 'on wobbly footing.' Tariffs still set to rise despite negotiations. Markets pricing 67% chance of September rate cut.

articles.headline: Big Tech AI Outlays Top $700 Billion, Pressure Mounts for Returns
articles.body: Big Tech's historic AI capital spending is set to surpass $700 billion this year, piling pressure on companies to show returns. Microsoft shares fell 23% in H1 2026, worst first half since 2022. Amazon and Meta have also laid off thousands. Memory's share of cloud capex hit 52% and could exceed 70% next year.

articles.headline: SpaceX Enters Nasdaq 100 as IPO Era Begins
articles.body: SpaceX officially entered the Nasdaq 100 index on Tuesday following its highly anticipated IPO. The listing caps a record-setting period for equity issuance. The SpaceX IPO is seen as one of the most significant market events of 2026.

articles.headline: Republicans Say It's Time Trump Change Course on SAVE America Act
articles.body: After two failed Senate votes, growing number of Republicans want Trump to pivot on the SAVE America Act voting legislation. GOP lawmakers frustrated as internal divisions are highlighted. House Republicans blocked by Anna Paulina Luna's protest from processing defense bill.

articles.headline: World Absorbs Historic Iran War Oil Supply Loss, But Depleted Stocks Bring Risks
articles.body: The world has absorbed the loss of over a billion barrels of oil supply since the Iran war began with surprising ease, but long-term peace remains elusive and buffer reserves are now drained. At its worst, the headline supply loss was 14 million barrels per day. Future price spike risk remains.

articles.headline: Democrats Look for Fighters in Trump's Second Midterm
articles.body: With midterm elections four months away, a new kind of Democratic candidate is ascendant: the outsider vowing to overhaul the system. Progressive populists have won key primaries. NYT/Siena poll found more than half of Democrats frustrated with the party. Party fractured over who should lead rebuilding.

articles.headline: A $1K Investment in Trump Accounts Could Be Worth $5,560 in 18 Years
articles.body: Trump Accounts launched nationwide on July 4. At 10% annual return, the $1,000 initial government deposit could grow to $5,560 in 18 years. With $25 monthly contributions and 8% returns, the account could reach $16,500. Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, BlackRock among firms offering $1,000 employer contributions.

articles.headline: Tech Leads Stock Futures Higher as AI Trade Faces Big Test
articles.body: Nasdaq 100 futures climbed 1.1% as chip stocks rebounded sharply. The AI trade faces a critical test with SK Hynix's US listing later this week and Samsung's record earnings expected today. The SOX semiconductor index had dropped 5.5% previously but showed signs of recovery. Mike Wilson of Morgan Stanley highlights hyperscalers driving memory demand.

articles.headline: Trump Heads to NATO Summit Amid Russia's Massive Attack on Ukraine
articles.body: World leaders gather in Turkey for a NATO summit as Russia carries out a massive attack on Ukraine killing at least 22. Trump to demand increased defense spending from European allies. Zelenskyy expected to press for more Patriot interceptor missiles.

articles.headline: Oil Drops as Hormuz Flows Return After Weekend U-Turns
articles.body: Oil prices fell as flows through the Strait of Hormuz returned after weekend diplomatic U-turns. OPEC+ reportedly likely to raise output quotas by 188,000 barrels per day for August. WTI trading near $68.55, Brent near $72. Gasoline prices declining as supply normalizes.

articles.headline: Microsoft Joins AI-Driven Tech Layoff Wave With 4,800 Job Cuts
articles.body: Microsoft's 4,800 job cuts mark the latest in a wave of tech layoffs as the company spends heavily on AI infrastructure. The software giant's shares fell 23% in H1 2026. Earlier this year, Microsoft offered voluntary buyouts to 9,000 employees. Azure growth strong but AI costs squeezing cash flows.

articles.headline: SK Hynix US Listing Draws $7 Billion Early Interest Ahead of Friday Debut
articles.body: SK Hynix launched a US share sale to raise 43 trillion won ($28 billion) and drew early indications of interest for up to $7 billion from major investors. The listing on Friday is expected to be one of the largest this year and could reshape the memory chip investment landscape.

articles.headline: Fed's Waller: Forward Guidance More Art Than Science
articles.body: Fed Governor Waller acknowledged forward guidance's value but warned it can hinder policymaking when inflexible. Pointed to 2021 delay as example. New Chair Warsh forming communications task force. FOMC minutes Wednesday will be first released under Warsh's tenure.

articles.headline: US Economy Rebounds But Frustration Lingers — What It Means
articles.body: The US added 172,000 jobs in May, unemployment held at 4.3%. But job openings surged unexpectedly and weekly claims hit a four-month high. Average long-term mortgage rate declined, providing relief to homebuyers. Mixed signals creating uncertainty for Fed policy.

articles.headline: Samsung Q2 Operating Profit Seen Topping 100 Trillion Won Before Bonus Provisions
articles.body: Samsung Electronics is estimated to have surpassed 100 trillion won in Q2 operating profit before accounting for semiconductor bonus provisions. Meritz Securities put Q2 operating profit at 90.1 trillion won. Full-year operating profit estimated at 380 trillion won, up 790% YoY. DS division president said 2026 profit would exceed 40 years of chip earnings combined.

articles.headline: Big Tech's $700 Billion AI Bet Faces Earnings Test This Week
articles.body: With Big Tech AI outlays set to top $700 billion this year, Q2 earnings season will be a critical test. Microsoft, expected to report later this month, forecast $190 billion in 2026 spending. JPMorgan warns memory's share of cloud capex reaching 52% this year may be unsustainable.

articles.headline: German Cabinet Turns Record Borrowing Into Defence-Budget Confrontation
articles.body: Germany's cabinet is in a standoff over the 2027 budget as record borrowing plans clash with demands for increased defense spending. The confrontation comes ahead of the NATO summit in Turkey where Trump is expected to pressure European allies to boost military outlays.

articles.headline: Week Ahead: FOMC Minutes, China Inflation, Canada Jobs, NATO Summit
articles.body: Key events this week: FOMC and ECB minutes, RBNZ rate decision, Chinese inflation data, Canada jobs report, and the NATO summit in Turkey. Markets also watching Samsung Q2 earnings and SK Hynix US listing. Fed minutes to provide insight into new Chair Warsh's policy stance.

articles.headline: EU Defence: Saving NATO in the Era of Trump
articles.body: The Financial Times editorial board argues that European nations must significantly increase defense capabilities as Trump questions NATO's value. The Ukraine war and Iran conflict have exposed Europe's military vulnerabilities. Germany and France under pressure to lead EU defense integration.

articles.headline: Chip Stocks Rally: AI Trade Revival or Dead Cat Bounce?
articles.body: Chip stocks rebounded sharply to start the week but analysts are divided on whether this is a sustainable recovery or a temporary bounce. The SOX index had dropped 5.5% previously. Samsung's record earnings today and SK Hynix's US listing on Friday will be crucial tests of AI demand sustainability.

articles.headline: In Trump's Second Midterm, Democrats Are Looking for Fighters
articles.body: A new kind of Democratic candidate is ascendant four months before the midterms: outsiders vowing to overhaul the system. Progressive populists win key primaries. NYT/Siena poll finds over half of Democrats frustrated with the party. Over 80% say the political and economic system needs to be torn down or overhauled.

articles.headline: Russia Hammers Kyiv With Missiles and Drones on Eve of NATO Summit
articles.body: Russia unleashed waves of 351 drones and 68 missiles at Ukraine, killing at least 22 people. All 29 ballistic missiles struck their targets, exposing gaps in Ukraine's air defenses. The attack came as NATO leaders prepared to gather in Turkey. Zelenskyy to press allies for more Patriot interceptors.

articles.headline: Trump Accounts Launch With Wall Street Ceremony, $800M First Week Inflow
articles.body: Trump Accounts officially launched July 4 with a historic Oval Office bell-ringing ceremony. Over 6 million accounts requested. $800 million in new capital to flow into markets this week. Michael Dell: 'Every newborn American child will start their life with $1,000 invested in America's greatest companies.'

articles.headline: Trump Posts Doctored Photo of Obamas and Air Force One With Graffiti
articles.body: President Trump posted a falsified image of the Obamas before an Air Force One spray-painted with graffiti including 'BLM' and Arabic script. The post followed a pattern of incendiary posts including one showing the Obamas as primates. Italian PM Meloni called Trump's similar post about her 'completely fabricated.'

articles.headline: Fed Grapples With Forward Guidance as Warsh Era Begins
articles.body: Fed Governor Waller acknowledged forward guidance both strengthens and hinders policymaking. New Chair Kevin Warsh forming a communications task force to suggest reforms by year-end. FOMC minutes Wednesday will be the first released under Warsh's leadership. Markets pricing 67% chance of September cut.

articles.headline: Republicans Plot Strategy to Fend Off Democratic Onslaught Against Trump Megabill
articles.body: Republicans plan to campaign on individual pieces of Trump's megabill that poll well while ignoring less popular provisions. The bill is projected to lead to 11.8 million fewer people having insurance by 2034 per CBO. GOP strategy includes accusing Democrats of voting to 'block tax cuts.' NRCC launching attack ads.

articles.headline: US Economy 'On Wobbly Footing' as Mixed Signals Cloud Outlook
articles.body: Comerica's Bill Adams: 'The economy is entering the second half of the year on wobbly footing.' First-quarter GDP revised down, PCE inflation ticked up, continued claims at highest since 2021. Bank of America's Bhave: 'We're at a fork in the road. If something is going to break, it's going to break soon.'

articles.headline: Microsoft Cuts 4,800 Jobs as AI Reshapes Tech Workforce
articles.body: Microsoft laid off 4,800 employees, about 2.1% of its workforce. 1,600 Xbox jobs cut immediately with total gaming reductions reaching 20% by fiscal year-end. Four Xbox studios being sold. Chief people officer Amy Coleman: 'the roles eliminated today are not being replaced by AI' but 'AI is changing how work gets done.'

articles.headline: Samsung Set to Report Record Profit as AI Chip Boom Intensifies
articles.body: Samsung Electronics' Q2 2026 operating profit expected around 86 trillion won, up approximately 1,740% YoY. Memory ASP increases of 40-60% QoQ. DS division president Kim Yong-kwan said 2026 profit would exceed cumulative 40 years of chip earnings. Samsung may surpass Nvidia as world's most profitable company by operating profit.

articles.headline: Historians Reject White House's Criticism of Smithsonian Museum
articles.body: Historians pushed back against White House criticism of the Smithsonian Institution's exhibits on American history. The controversy is part of broader cultural battles as the midterm elections approach. Trump has stepped up efforts to portray Democrats as extremists.

articles.headline: Prince Harry Insiders Slam Royals for Disinviting Him From Buckingham Palace
articles.body: Prince Harry's representatives criticized the royal family for disinviting him from Buckingham Palace during his UK visit. Insiders questioned: 'How is there not one room?' The ongoing rift between Harry and the royal family continues to make headlines.

articles.headline: Gustavo Dudamel's Final Hollywood Bowl Concert to Benefit Venezuela After Earthquakes
articles.body: Conductor Gustavo Dudamel's final Hollywood Bowl concert will raise funds for Venezuela following devastating earthquakes. The concert marks the end of his tenure with the LA Philharmonic before taking a new position.

articles.headline: State of Decay 3 Might No Longer Launch Day One on Xbox Game Pass
articles.body: Amid Microsoft's major Xbox restructuring including 20% staff cuts and studio selloffs, reports suggest State of Decay 3 may not launch day one on Xbox Game Pass. The changes signal a strategic shift in Microsoft's gaming business model.

articles.headline: Trump Posts Doctored Photo of Obamas and Air Force One With Graffiti
articles.body: President Trump posted a falsified image of Barack and Michelle Obama before an Air Force One spray-painted with graffiti including 'BLM' and Arabic script. Follows a pattern of incendiary posts including one showing Obamas as primates. Italian PM Meloni called a similar post about her 'completely fabricated.'

articles.headline: Russia Kills at Least 22 in Massive Attack on Kyiv Region Ahead of NATO Summit
articles.body: Russia fired 351 drones and 68 missiles at Ukraine, all 29 ballistic missiles struck targets. 15 killed in Kyiv, 7 more in wider region. Attack exposes gaps in air defenses. Zelenskyy to press NATO allies for more Patriot interceptors at Turkey summit.

articles.headline: Dow Surges Past 53,000 as Tech Stocks Rebound on AI Optimism
articles.body: The Dow closed at a record 53,032.55 on Monday. S&P 500 rose 0.72%, Nasdaq climbed 1.12%. The rally was driven by renewed confidence in AI-related stocks after a late-June selloff. Broadcom expanded its Apple partnership through 2031. Q2 earnings season seen as next catalyst.

articles.headline: Trump Accounts Draw 6 Million Signups in First Days
articles.body: Over 6 million Trump Accounts requested within days of launch, with 86% from families earning under $200,000. $800 million in new capital to be invested this week. Michael and Susan Dell pledged $6.25 billion for $250 contributions to 25 million children. 50+ companies committed to employer contributions.

articles.headline: US-Iran Talks Resume in Doha as Temporary Ceasefire Holds
articles.body: US and Iranian negotiators resumed talks in Doha focusing on management of the Strait of Hormuz and terms of a 60-day MOU aimed at a comprehensive nuclear deal. A temporary one-week de-escalation agreement is in place. US officials warn military option remains on the table if talks fail.

articles.headline: Fed's Waller Says Forward Guidance Both Helps and Hinders Policy
articles.body: Fed Governor Waller acknowledged forward guidance accelerated policy transmission in 2021 when 2-year yields rose 200bps before any rate hike. But he also cited 2021 delay as example of guidance tying FOMC's hands. New Fed Chair Warsh forming communications task force to recommend reforms by year-end.

articles.headline: Microsoft Slashes 4,800 Jobs, Sells Xbox Studios in Major Restructuring
articles.body: Microsoft cutting 4,800 jobs globally, including 600 in Washington state. Xbox reducing headcount by 20% (~3,200 jobs) and selling four studios. CEO Asha Sharma: 'most significant restructure in Xbox history.' Stock down 30% over 9 months, erasing $1.2 trillion in market value. AI infrastructure spending driving cost pressures.

articles.headline: Samsung Q2 Earnings: The $100 Trillion Won Question
articles.body: Samsung's DS division president Kim Yong-kwan said 2026 operating profit will exceed cumulative 40 years of chip earnings combined. Analysts expect Q2 operating profit around 84.6 trillion won. Before bonus provisions, could top 100 trillion won for first time. Samsung may surpass Nvidia as world's most profitable company.

articles.headline: OPEC+ Likely to Raise Output by 188,000 Barrels Per Day for August
articles.body: OPEC+ is reportedly likely to raise oil output quotas for August by 188,000 barrels per day at the Sunday meeting. The move comes as oil prices have returned to pre-Iran war levels with the Strait of Hormuz reopened. Decision reflects improving supply outlook.

articles.headline: Republicans in Disarray as SAVE America Act Stalls Again
articles.body: After two failed Senate votes on the SAVE America Act, Republican frustration grows. Rep. Anna Paulina Luna froze the House floor last week, preventing defense bill processing. Speaker Mike Johnson sent members home early for July Fourth recess. GOP lawmakers increasingly want Trump to pivot to achievable legislation.

articles.headline: Global Chip Stocks: AI Boom or Bubble? Earnings Season to Decide
articles.body: This week's earnings from Samsung and events including SK Hynix's US listing will test whether the AI chip boom is sustainable. JPMorgan questions whether memory's rising share of cloud capex (52% in 2026, potentially 70%+ in 2027) can continue. Citi raised Samsung target to 530,000 won, implying 71% upside.

articles.headline: Trump's Face to Appear on Money, Passports and Cards
articles.body: President Trump has branded his face, name and signature on an expanding array of government documents and currency. The branding push is part of a broader effort to cement his legacy during his second term. Critics call it unprecedented self-promotion using government resources.

articles.headline: Judge Appointed Last Year Is Overseeing Hearing in Killing of Charlie Kirk
articles.body: A judge appointed just last year is overseeing the hearing in the high-profile killing of conservative activist Charlie Kirk. The case has drawn national attention amid the charged political climate ahead of the midterm elections.

articles.headline: Russia Hammers Kyiv With Missiles and Drones on Eve of NATO Summit
articles.body: Russia unleashed massive missile and drone attacks on Ukraine killing at least 22 in the Kyiv region. All 29 ballistic missiles struck their targets. The attack comes as NATO leaders prepare to gather in Turkey. Zelenskyy expected to press for more air defense systems.

articles.headline: Halftime for the Markets: AI Rally Faces Critical Earnings Test
articles.body: Global markets enter H2 2026 at a crossroads. The AI-driven rally that powered record highs faces its biggest test as Q2 earnings season begins. Samsung's expected record profit and SK Hynix's US listing will be closely watched. Fed under new Chair Warsh releases first FOMC minutes Wednesday.

articles.headline: Chip Stocks Rally as AI Fears Ease, Dow Hits 53,000
articles.body: Wall Street surged Monday with Dow closing above 53,000 for the first time. S&P 500 rose 0.72%, Nasdaq climbed 1.12%. Broadcom expanded Apple chip partnership through 2031. SK Hynix drew $7 billion early interest for US listing. Oil flat at pre-Iran war levels.

articles.headline: Saving NATO in the Era of Trump: EU Defence Spending Under Scrutiny
articles.body: FT editorial: European nations must significantly increase defence capabilities as Trump questions NATO's value at the Turkey summit. The Ukraine war and Iran conflict have exposed Europe's military vulnerabilities. Germany and France face pressure to lead EU defence integration.

articles.headline: World Absorbs Historic Iran War Oil Supply Loss, But Depleted Stocks Bring Risks
articles.body: The world has absorbed with surprising ease the loss of over a billion barrels of oil supply since the Iran war began. At its worst, supply loss was 14 million barrels per day. But buffer reserves are now drained. Long-term peace elusive. Future price spike risk remains despite oil returning to pre-war levels.

articles.headline: Fed's Waller Opens Door to Rethinking Forward Guidance
articles.body: Fed Governor Waller said forward guidance 'more art than science,' citing both successes and failures. The 2021 delay in rate increases was a key failure as inflation surged. New Chair Warsh forming a task force of outside experts to review Fed communications by year-end. First FOMC minutes under Warsh due Wednesday.

articles.headline: Big Tech's $700 Billion AI Bet: Earnings Season to Test Return on Investment
articles.body: Big Tech AI capital spending set to top $700 billion in 2026. Microsoft forecast $190 billion in spending. Memory's share of cloud capex reached 52% this year, could exceed 70% in 2027. JPMorgan questions sustainability. Microsoft shares down 23% in H1, worst since 2022.

articles.headline: German Cabinet Budget Standoff Clouds EU Defence Outlook
articles.body: Germany's cabinet locked in confrontation over 2027 budget as record borrowing plans clash with defence spending demands. The standoff comes at a sensitive time with Trump expected to pressure NATO allies at the Turkey summit. EU defence integration efforts face headwinds.

articles.headline: Samsung on Track to Surpass Nvidia as World's Most Profitable Company
articles.body: Samsung DS division president said 2026 profit will exceed all chip earnings from ~40 years combined. Q2 operating profit expected around 84.6 trillion won (~$55.1 billion), potentially surpassing Nvidia's $53.54 billion Q1 figure. Memory prices up 90% in Q1, 50-60% in Q2, with further ~20% hike negotiated for Q3.

articles.headline: An American Birthday Worth Questioning: FT View on US at 250
articles.body: FT editorial reflects on America's 250th anniversary. While the economy shows resilience with record stock markets and low unemployment, political polarization, Trump's divisive rhetoric, and questions about democratic institutions cloud the celebration. Trump Accounts launch seen as politically motivated wealth transfer.

articles.headline: Week Ahead: FOMC Minutes, NATO Summit, Samsung Earnings, SK Hynix Listing
articles.body: Key events: FOMC minutes (Wednesday), NATO summit in Turkey, Samsung Q2 earnings (Tuesday), SK Hynix Nasdaq listing (Friday), China inflation data, Canada jobs report. Fed Chair Warsh's first minutes will be closely parsed. NATO summit comes amid Russia's intensified attacks on Ukraine.

articles.headline: Market Moves: Themes That Mattered in June 2026
articles.body: June saw mixed global equity performance with Europe outperforming. The AI trade showed divergence as semiconductor stocks benefited while hyperscalers came under pressure. Oil fell ~20% after US-Iran MOU. Sector rotation evident with value beating growth. Gold fell ~12% on dollar strength and hawkish Fed repricing.

articles.headline: US Economy on Wobbly Footing: Why Strategists Are Cautious
articles.body: Despite record highs, Wall Street strategists signal caution. JPMorgan warns against getting 'carried away by inflation momentum.' Comerica's Adams: 'economy entering H2 on wobbly footing.' First-quarter GDP revised down, PCE inflation ticked up, continued claims at highest since 2021. Markets pricing 67% chance of September Fed cut.
